headline: Türkiye markets stay in a tight-policy holding pattern as CBRT keeps rates at 37% and the long-end rallies, with the lira still shadowed by domestic political-legal risk.
summary: Sentiment is defined by the CBRT's restrictive-for-longer stance after it held the policy rate at 37% for a third straight meeting, citing energy-driven inflation risks and lira defence. Rates have grceeled at the long end, with the 10-year local yield down nearly 5 points over the past month to ~30.8%, even as USDTRY trades above 43. Lira stability remains sensitive to domestic politics after courts moved against opposition leader Özel. Available real-time results yielded few fresh, individually-datable primary disclosures (KAP/CBRT/Treasury) within the last 24 hours, so this briefing is deliberately limited to verifiable items rather than padded with filler.

items.title: CBRT holds policy rate at 37% for a third straight meeting, reaffirms restrictive stance
items.summary: The Central Bank of the Republic of Türkiye kept its policy rate at 37%, citing energy-driven inflation risk and pledging to keep policy restrictive until disinflation is secured.

items.title: Turkish 10-year local government bond yield eases to ~30.8%
items.summary: Türkiye's 10-year government bond yield stood at 30.83%, down roughly 4.9 points over the past month despite remaining above year-ago levels.

items.title: Lira under intermittent pressure on domestic political-legal risk
items.summary: The CBRT reiterated it will defend lira stability, which was recently tested after Turkish courts moved against opposition leader Özel, with USDTRY trading above 43.

items.title: Treasury central government debt stock at TL13,325bn, over half FX-denominated
items.source: Republic of Türkiye Ministry of Treasury and Finance
items.summary: Central government debt stock was TL13,325bn, of which TL7,050bn was FX-denominated, underscoring continued sovereign hard-currency funding and hedging needs.
